raw_consumer_statement:
Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, three recurring charges of {$690.00} each were posted to my Barclaycard account by XXXX XXXX XXXX, totaling {$2000.00}. These charges were not authorized. 

The merchant submitted a contract during the dispute process that was not signed by the cardholder and instead listed a third party as the buyer. The cardholder never authorized recurring billing, a monthly membership, or an 11-month term.

Written cancellation was sent to the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX, prior to all disputed charges. Despite cancellation, charges continued on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. Follow-up refund requests were sent on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, with no response from the merchant. 

A dispute and subsequent second-level review were submitted to Barclays. Barclays denied the dispute, stating the merchant did not violate the contract, without identifying any authorization linking the cardholder to recurring charges.
